alloy_matches.reason: Same alloy name and composition (FeAl, 50 at.% each).
Same alloy name and composition (MnAl, 50 at.% each).

process_matches.reason: Both describe first-principles DFT calculations with same methods (CASTEP, GGA-PBE, 450 eV cutoff, 8x8x8 k-points) on the same alloy system.

truth.papers.A69.title: Properties of Fe–Mn–Al alloys with different Mn contents using density functional theory
truth.papers.A69.paper_scope: First-principles DFT investigation of chemical stability, electronic structures, mechanical properties, and Debye temperature of Fe1-xMnxAl alloys to obtain complete data for design of high-temperature structural and functional materials.

prediction.papers.A69.title: Properties of Fe–Mn–Al alloys with different Mn contents using density functional theory
prediction.papers.A69.paper_scope: First-principles DFT investigation of chemical stability, electronic structures, mechanical properties and Debye temperature of Fe-Mn-Al alloys with varying Mn content.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe.alloys_notes: Pure Fe reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.
truth.alloys.alloy_mn.alloys_notes: Pure Mn reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.
truth.alloys.alloy_al.alloys_notes: Pure Al reference calculated for cohesion and formation enthalpy baseline.

prediction.alloys.alloy_feal.alloys_notes: B2 ordered cubic structure; x=0 end member of the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl series.
prediction.alloys.alloy_mnal.alloys_notes: B2 ordered cubic structure; x=1 end member of the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl series.

truth.processes.proc_first_principles.descript: First-principles DFT calculations using CASTEP code with GGA-PBE, ultrasoft pseudopotentials, plane-wave cutoff 450 eV, Monkhorst-Pack 8x8x8 k-point mesh; geometry optimization of Fe1-xMnxAl cubic supercells.

prediction.processes.proc_first_principles.descript: First-principles density functional theory (DFT) calculations using the CASTEP code with ultrasoft pseudopotentials, GGA-PBE exchange-correlation functional, Monkhorst-Pack 8×8×8 k-point mesh, and a plane-wave cutoff energy of 450 eV.
prediction.processes.proc_first_principles.processe: Geometry optimization converged to 2×10^{-6} eV total energy and 0.5 eV·nm^{-1} force per atom.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: First-principles DFT calculations implemented in CASTEP
truth.computational_details.idx_0.potential_mo: Ultrasoft pseudopotentials with GGA-PBE exchange-correlation functional
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated lattice parameters and properties compared favorably with available experimental and theoretical data (see Table 1 and text).
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Used to evaluate thermodynamic stability of Fe1-xMnxAl alloys from total energies and bulk reference energies.
Used to evaluate thermodynamic stability from total energies and isolated atom energies.
Voigt-Reuss-Hill (VRH) approximation for polycrystalline elastic moduli
Combines Voigt and Reuss averages to calculate bulk modulus B, shear modulus G, Young's modulus E, and Poisson's ratio from single-crystal elastic constants C11, C12, C44.
Hv = 2(k^2 G)^{0.585} - 3, with k = G/B, giving hardness from shear modulus and Pugh ratio.
Includes universal anisotropic index A^U, anisotropy of bulk modulus A_B, shear modulus A_G, and shear anisotropic factors A1, A2, A3 for (100), (010), (001) planes.
Debye temperature and sound velocity calculation
Calculates Debye temperature and longitudinal, transverse, and average sound velocities from elastic moduli and mass density.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: First-principles density functional theory (DFT) with plane-wave basis set
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated lattice parameters and elastic properties show good agreement with previous experimental and calculated values reported in the literature
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Calculation of polycrystalline bulk modulus, shear modulus, Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio from single-crystal elastic constants
Standard averaging method for polycrystalline mechanical properties
Empirical estimation of Vickers hardness from bulk and shear moduli
Calculation of Debye temperature and average sound velocity from elastic constants and density
Includes longitudinal, transverse and average sound velocities
Mechanical stability criteria for cubic crystals

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fe–Mn–Al alloys exhibit a combination of covalent and metallic bonding, which results in good electronic conductivity, relatively high melting points and high hardness.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: FeAl is the most stable alloy in the Fe1-xMnxAl system based on formation enthalpy and cohesive energy.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: MnAl exhibits the largest elastic anisotropy among Fe–Mn–Al alloys (A^U = 3.80), while FeAl has the smallest (A^U = 0.56).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Among mixed Fe-Mn-Al compounds, Fe0.5Mn0.5Al has the largest elastic anisotropy (A^U = 2.06).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The Debye temperature decreases from FeAl to Fe0.5Mn0.5Al with increasing Mn content, then increases to Fe0.25Mn0.75Al before decreasing again to MnAl.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The change in hardness and Debye temperature follows a similar trend, likely due to bonding features.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: FeAl and Fe0.875Mn0.125Al are brittle (B/G < 1.75), while all other Fe–Mn–Al alloys studied are ductile.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The Fe–Mn–Al system exhibits potential as high-temperature structural materials, high-strength materials, and functional materials according to the introduction and conclusions.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fig. 9 shows planar projections of Young's modulus on (001) and (110) planes, indicating strong directional dependence and anisotropy; pure-text pipeline cannot read exact angular values from the polar plots.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Formation enthalpy and cohesive energy both increase monotonically with increasing Mn content across the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl series; FeAl (x=0) possesses the lowest formation enthalpy (-0.391 eV·atom^{-1}) and is the most thermodynamically stable alloy.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Chemical bonding analysis indicates that all Fe-Mn-Al alloys exhibit a combination of covalent and metallic bonds, leading to predicted good electronic conductivity, high melting points and high hardness.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Brittle-to-ductile behavior predicted: FeAl and Fe_{0.875}Mn_{0.125}Al are brittle (B/G = 1.56 and 1.75, respectively), while all other compositions with higher Mn content are ductile (B/G > 1.75).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: MnAl exhibits the strongest elastic anisotropy among the studied alloys (universal anisotropic index A^U = 3.80), whereas FeAl is the most isotropic (A^U = 0.56).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al shows anomalous extrema including the lowest Young's modulus (202.1 GPa), lowest shear modulus (77.2 GPa), lowest Debye temperature (533.3 K), and highest Poisson's ratio (0.309) and B/G ratio (2.28), indicating the highest ductility and compressibility among the intermediate compounds.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Figure 9 illustrates planar projections of Young's modulus on (001) and (110) crystallographic planes showing strong directional dependence and disparate anisotropy; detailed numerical angular dependencies are not provided in the text.
